item.title: India Rate-Hike Bets Build as Pricey Oil, Weak Rupee Stoke Inflation Risks
item.description: Most economists expect the RBI to hold today, though an August hike looks near certain.

item.title: China Stock-Yuan Correlation Hits Three-Year High on Optimism
item.description: Chinese stocks and the yuan are moving increasingly in tandem as sentiment toward the country’s assets improves, driven by enthusiasm over artificial intelligence and investors’ push for diversification.

item.title: Iron Ore Futures Head for Fourth Weekly Loss on Seasonal Lull
item.description: Iron ore headed for a fourth straight weekly loss as prices retreat on a seasonally weakening market.

item.title: Ex-Coup Boss Tightens Grip on Guinea With Parliamentary Vote Win
item.description: Guinean President Mamadi Doumbouya has strengthened his hold on power after his party won most of the seats in the May 31 parliamentary elections.

item.title: Amundi Says Asia Tech Rally Faces Fed Risk But No Bubble Yet
item.description: Asia’s AI-led stock boom has room to run further unless a shift in US interest-rate expectations rattles the hyperscalers underpinning the investment cycle, according to Amundi, Europe’s largest asset manager.

item.title: SpaceX Boosts Japan Fundraising Goal to $2.5 Billion in Mega IPO
item.description: SpaceX has increased the amount it’s planning to raise from the Japanese portion of its share sale by a quarter, indicating strong demand among the country’s retail investors.

item.title: Jan de Nul Wins Contract to Dig Argentina’s $10 Billion Waterway
item.description: Belgian dredger Jan de Nul NV and local partner Servimagnus SA won a 25-year contract on Thursday from Argentina’s government to upgrade the nation’s chief trade route in a concession overshadowed by accusations of Chinese influence.

item.title: Indonesia Denies Finance Chief Purbaya to Go as Markets Slump
item.description: Indonesian Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa denied he is resigning from office, after rumors of his departure swept through slumping markets this week.

item.title: Dajin Heavy Drops in Hong Kong Debut After $847 Million Listing
item.description: Shares of wind-power equipment maker Dajin Heavy Industry traded lower in their Hong Kong debut on Friday, following the biggest listing in the city in more than six weeks.

item.title: Philippines to Sell Up to 25B Pesos Treasury Bond Due Nov. 2034
item.description: The Philippines Bureau of the Treasury in Manila said it will sell up to 25 billion pesos ($406.2 million) treasury bonds on June 9.

item.title: Philippine Inflation Slows as Transport Cost Pressures Ease
item.description: Philippine inflation unexpectedly decelerated in May as easing transport costs took pressure off soaring prices triggered by the Iran war.

item.title: Broadcom Is Eschewing Acquisitions in Favor of AI Organic Growth
item.description: Broadcom Inc. Chief Executive Officer Hock Tan, who created the chip behemoth using a series of acquisitions, said the company is now less focused on dealmaking because AI offers stronger growth potential.

item.title: Korea’s Current Account Surplus Stays Near Record on Chip Boom
item.description: South Korea’s current-account surplus remained near record levels in April, underscoring the strength of the country’s semiconductor-driven export boom.

item.title: China Speeds Past Japan in Australian Car Imports on EV Bonanza
item.description: China has surged past Japan to become the biggest car supplier to Australia, with imports from companies such as BYD Co. accelerating to a record high, powered by demand for electric vehicles.

item.title: T&D Will Use Funds From Sale of Insurance Unit for Buybacks
item.description: T&D Holdings Inc. plans to use the roughly ¥188 billion ($1.2 billion) it will get from the sale of a life insurance subsidiary for share buybacks and strategic investments, including in artificial intelligence.

item.title: Meta AI Chief Sees Opportunity in Models’ Giving Health Advice
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. Chief AI Officer Alexandr Wang said the company’s future artificial intelligence models will differentiate themselves from competitors through their consumer health capabilities.

item.title: Fuel Shocks, Rising Prices Threaten India’s Consumption Growth
item.description: The global energy crisis is threatening to slow spending among India’s vast middle class, undermining a fragile recovery in consumption and adding to the woes of an economy already grappling with a markets and currency slump.

item.title: Vingroup’s 1,000% Surge Tests Limits of Vietnam’s Market Boom
item.description: Blistering gains in Vietnam’s biggest stock have confounded seasoned analysts and set off a frenzy among retail traders, raising doubts about how long the rally can last.

item.title: IPOs, Huawei Plan Add to China’s $900 Billion Chip Stock Boom
item.description: The rally in China’s semiconductor stocks is poised to extend thanks to upcoming blockbuster IPOs and technology breakthroughs, investors and analysts say.

item.title: ‘Sell Indonesia’ Sweeps Trading Desks as Prabowo Tightens Grip
item.description: Global investors are rapidly losing confidence in Indonesia as the nation’s stocks tumble at the fastest pace worldwide and its currency sinks to all-time lows.

item.title: A Little-Known Indian Stock’s 530% Rally Shows Hidden AI Winners
item.description: The overarching narrative is that Indian equity markets missed out on the global artificial intelligence boom. But a look under the hood reveals a slew of smaller firms winning from trillions of dollars being spent on AI capacity.

item.title: Japan Likely Sold Foreign Securities to Fund FX Intervention
item.description: Japan’s holdings of foreign securities fell sharply in May, signaling that the government may have tapped overseas assets, including US Treasuries, to finance its record currency market intervention over the past month.

item.title: Japan’s Real Wages Rise for Fourth Month, Backing BOJ Hike
item.description: Japanese workers’ real wages increased for a fourth month, marking the longest stretch of gains in four years and bolstering the case for the Bank of Japan to raise interest rates this month.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Uncertainty Surrounds Progress in US-Iran Talks
item.description: Gold was little changed as uncertainty persisted over progress in US-Iran talks to end the war that’s roiled global markets.

item.title: Sempra Unit Starts Producing LNG at New Export Plant in Mexico
item.description: Sempra Infrastructure said it began producing liquefied natural gas from Mexico’s first West Coast export terminal, with plans to ultimately ship the fuel to Asia in a move that could add relief to a market hurt by tightened global supply due to the Iran war.

item.title: Cracks Emerge in Kospi’s 105% Rally as Breadth Concerns Mount
item.description: South Korea’s $4.9 trillion stock market is starting to flash signs of strain after a world-beating rally.

item.title: Wall Street Sees SpaceX’s AI Revenue 100 Times Higher by 2030
item.description: Research analysts across Wall Street are telling would-be SpaceX IPO buyers that they’re modeling for the company’s artificial intelligence division to see 100 times revenue growth at the end of the decade, to help justify a targeted $1.8 trillion valuation.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Dip as AI Rally Stalls, Oil Steady: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia looked set to edge lower on Friday as investors grappled with a pullback in artificial-intelligence shares and hopes for a diplomatic resolution to tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: CME’s Duffy Voices ‘Grave Concerns’ With New Perps Contracts
item.description: Terry Duffy, the chief executive officer of CME Group Inc., said he is “very concerned” about the rise of so-called perpetual futures, an increasingly popular kind of financial contract that recently won key US regulatory approval.

item.title: Oil Steadies After First Drop This Week on Peace Talk Optimism
item.description: Oil was steady after its first decline this week on optimism that ceasefire talks between the US and Iran will see energy flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.
